blocks.content:
Grafana Knowledge Graph automatically discovers and maps every entity in your environment -- services, nodes, pods, and more. In this guide you will explore two key features:

- **Entity catalog** -- browse, search, and inspect all discovered entities
- **RCA workbench** -- investigate issues across entities using insights and multiple visualization modes
You will learn how to browse discovered entities, filter by type and health, and drill into service-level details.
Navigate to the **Entity catalog**.

blocks.tooltip: Insight rings visualize health across five categories: Saturation, Amend, Anomaly, Failure, and Error.
blocks.content: Each entity in the catalog has insight rings -- colored circles that show its health status at a glance. The table also shows the entity name, technology stack, and key metrics like **Latency P95**, **Error Ratio**, and **Request Rate**.

blocks.tooltip: Search narrows the catalog to matching entities.
blocks.content: Use the search box to quickly find a specific entity by name.

### Entity types

The left sidebar lets you filter entities by type. The Knowledge Graph discovers many types of entities from your telemetry data.

blocks.tooltip: Services are the default view -- these are the application-level entities discovered from your metrics and traces.
blocks.content: **Service** is selected by default. Services represent application-level components discovered from metrics and traces. The count shows how many entities of each type exist.

blocks.tooltip: Switch to see infrastructure nodes instead of services.
blocks.content: Click **Node** to view infrastructure-level entities. Nodes represent the underlying compute instances running your services.

blocks.tooltip: Switch back to the service view.
blocks.content: Click **Service** to return to the service view.

### Insight rings

The colored rings next to each entity name represent its health status across five categories. These are the same insight categories used throughout Knowledge Graph.

blocks.tooltip: Filter to only show entities with error insights.
blocks.content:
Check **Error** under Insight Rings to filter the catalog to only entities experiencing errors. The five insight categories are:

- **Saturation** -- resource limits being reached
- **Amend** -- configuration changes detected
- **Anomaly** -- unusual metric behavior
- **Failure** -- component failures
- **Error** -- application errors

blocks.tooltip: Clear the filter to show all entities again.
blocks.content: Check **All** to clear the insight filter and show all entities.

### Drill into an entity

Clicking an entity name opens a detailed drawer with deep observability data for that entity.

blocks.tooltip: Opens the entity detail drawer with metrics, logs, traces, and more.
blocks.content: Click any entity name to open the detail drawer. This gives you instant access to **Latency**, **Error Ratio**, **Request Rate** charts, plus tabs for **Logs**, **Traces**, **Profiles**, **Metrics**, **Kubernetes** details, and **Service KPI**.
Close the entity detail drawer.
You learned how to browse the Entity catalog, filter by entity type and insight category, and drill into individual entities for detailed observability data.
You will learn how to use the RCA workbench to investigate issues across multiple entities using search, timelines, graphs, and mind maps.
Navigate to the **RCA workbench**.
The RCA (Root Cause Analysis) workbench is your investigation hub. You add entities here to compare their insights side-by-side and trace the chain of causation across your environment.

blocks.tooltip: Quickly populates the workbench with services that have the most active insights.
blocks.content: Click **Add top services by insights** to automatically populate the workbench with the services that currently have the most active issues. This is the fastest way to start an investigation.

### Timeline view

The Timeline is the default view. It shows a time-based visualization of when insights fired for each entity -- red bars for errors, orange for warnings.

blocks.tooltip: Shows when insights fired over time for each entity.
blocks.content: The **Timeline** tab displays a chronological view of insights per entity. Red bars indicate errors, orange bars indicate warnings. Use this to spot when issues started and whether they correlate across services.

### Summary view

The Summary view groups insights by entity so you can quickly see what is affecting each service.

blocks.tooltip: Lists each entity with its specific active insights expanded.
blocks.content: Click **Summary** to see each entity with its specific insights listed below it. This view tells you exactly what assertions are firing -- for example, an error like `EventStreamError` or an anomaly like `LatencyAverageAnomaly`.

### Graph view

The Graph view shows the topology of your entities and how they connect to each other.

blocks.tooltip: Visualizes entity relationships and dependencies as a network graph.
blocks.content: Click **Graph** to see a visual topology of the entities in your workbench. Nodes are colored by health status -- green for healthy, red for critical. This helps you understand blast radius and dependency chains.

### Mind map view

The Mind map provides a hierarchical breakdown of all insights by category and severity.

blocks.tooltip: Breaks down insights into a hierarchical tree by category.
blocks.content: Click **Mind map** to see a tree-structured breakdown of all insights. The root node shows total counts (critical, warning, info), which branch into categories like **error** and **anomaly**. This gives you an at-a-glance severity summary.

blocks.tooltip: Return to the timeline to continue your investigation.
blocks.content: Click **Timeline** to return to the chronological view.

### Search for entities

You can search for specific entity types or names to add them to the workbench.

blocks.tooltip: Type queries like 'Show all Services' or search for specific entity names.
blocks.content: The search bar accepts natural-language-style queries like **Show all Services** or **Show all Nodes**. You can also search for specific entity names. The workbench supports up to 20 entities at a time.
You learned how to populate the RCA workbench, read timeline insights, switch between Summary, Graph, and Mind map views, and search for entities to investigate.

You have completed the Knowledge Graph tour. You now know how to:

- Browse and filter entities in the **Entity catalog**
- Drill into individual entities for detailed metrics, logs, and traces
- Use the **RCA workbench** to investigate issues across multiple entities
- Read insights across Timeline, Summary, Graph, and Mind map views

Next, try using the **Entity Graph** (found in the Observability menu) for a full visual topology of your environment, or set up **Health targets** to define SLOs for your services.
